input.headline: Wicked: For Good saved by the magnetic performances of its stars

raw.headline: Wicked: For Good saved by the magnetic performances of its stars

winner: The actors' performances were so magnetic that the audience was physically pulled into the orchestra pit, making it impossible to leave until the final curtain call.

candidates.text: I saw a play called "Wicked: For Good," and I was magnet-ically impressed.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Puns on 'magnetic' and 'Wicked: For Good' with clever wordplay and specificity."}

candidates.text: Why did the wicked witch need a magnet? Because her charm was so strong, she could attract any performance!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat clever with the wordplay on 'magnet' and 'charm,' but it's a bit forced and lacks specificity to the headline. It doesn't fully tie into the context of the performances or the show itself."}

candidates.text: The actors were so magnetic that they didn't just save the show; they practically pulled the audience out of their seats by force.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Puns on 'magnetic' are clever and specific, with a surprising twist that ties back to the headline."}

candidates.text: The cast of *Wicked* performed so magnetically that one audience member was last seen crawling toward the stage, metal spoon in hand, trying to scrape off his new nickname.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Surprising and specific twist with the audience member and metal spoon, ties back to 'magnetically' in a clever way."}

candidates.text: The actors' performances were so magnetic that the audience didn't just feel the emotion; they were physically pulled into the front row and stuck to the seats.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Creative twist on 'magnetic performances,' with physical humor and specificity that ties back to the headline."}

candidates.text: The actors' performances were so magnetic that the audience was physically pulled into the orchestra pit, making it impossible to leave until the final curtain call.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Surprising and specific metaphor with a touch of absurdity"}

candidates.text: The play was so good, it magnetically pulled in audience members from across the country—some even had to be rescued by the fire department.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 7.5, "reason": "The joke is clever with the wordplay on 'magnetically pulled in' and the unexpected twist about the fire department. It's specific to the headline and concise, though the explanation of the rescue feels slightly forced."}

candidates.text: The show *Wicked* was so bad, even the witches' brooms were better at flying—turns out, their performances were the only thing holding this "for good" story together.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat surprising and specific to the show, but the humor feels a bit forced with the broom comparison. It's not offensive or generic, but lacks the sharp punch that would elevate it to a higher score."}

candidates.text: The play was so bad, even the magnets had to be dragged offstage.
